mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of setup for distributed systems.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a managed Kubernetes cluster with the required specifications.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the Ready state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the vLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-deployment.yaml

```

> This command deploys the vLLM application.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command enables autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Deploy monitoring resources.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the deployment.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/kubernetes/vllm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will delete the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Ensure you back up any important data before upgrading. This process includes draining nodes, upgrading the deployment, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl set image deployment/vllm vllm=<new-image>:<new-tag>

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to drain.
> Specify the new image and tag for the upgrade.
> Bring the node back online after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running with the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review application logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust resource requests/limits.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'NodeNotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes in the cluster are not in a ready state.

**Fix:**
Check node status with 'kubectl get nodes' and investigate node conditions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'FailedScheduling'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There are insufficient resources available to schedule the pod.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources or adjust the resource requests in your deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new decoding algorithms and improved memory management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.17.0, ensure to update your model configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing all pods in the Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
